message: Node started: Problem Class: Minimum Spanning Tree / Steiner Tree variant on a 2D grid with directed monotonicity constraints. Mathematical Objective: Minimize the total cost C, where C is the sum of Manhattan distances of all operations used to reach all N target nodes from the origin (0,0). Structure: A directed acyclic graph (DAG) where nodes are (x, y) coordinates. Each edge (fx, fy) to (tx, ty) must satisfy tx >= fx and ty >= fy. State-Transition Rules: A new node can only be created if its source node already exists in the set of created nodes. The initial node is (0,0). Input Specification: An integer N representing the number of target coordinates, followed by N pairs of integers (ai, bi). Output Specification: An integer m representing the number of operations, followed by m lines each containing four integers: from_x, from_y, to_x, to_y. Critical Properties: The solution must ensure all N target points are included in the set of created nodes. The cost function is additive based on Manhattan distance. The problem is equivalent to finding a minimum cost directed tree rooted at (0,0) that spans all target points under the constraint of non-decreasing coordinates.
